Operation 'secretmanager.projects.secrets.testIamPermissions' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_output_details. The operation lacks a summary entirely. The description is mostly clear but could be improved for AI agent interpretability by: (1) explicitly stating it returns a TestIamPermissionsResponse with permission list, (2) clarifying the caller context (authenticated user), (3) reorganizing for better readability, and (4) emphasizing the non-authoritative nature more clearly. The summary should be action-oriented and concise to help agents understand this is a permission-checking operation.

data.current_description: Returns permissions that a caller has for the specified secret. If the secret does not exist, this call returns an empty set of permissions, not a NOT_FOUND error. Note: This operation is designed to be used for building permission-aware UIs and command-line tools, not for authorization checking. This operation may "fail open" without warning.
data.summary_suggestion: Test IAM permissions on a secret
data.description_suggestion: Tests which IAM permissions the authenticated caller has on the specified secret resource. Returns a list of permissions the caller possesses. If the secret does not exist, returns an empty set of permissions rather than a NOT_FOUND error. This operation is intended for building permission-aware UIs and command-line tools, not for authorization enforcement. Note: This operation may fail open without warning.
data.reasoning: The operation lacks a summary entirely. The description is mostly clear but could be improved for AI agent interpretability by: (1) explicitly stating it returns a TestIamPermissionsResponse with permission list, (2) clarifying the caller context (authenticated user), (3) reorganizing for better readability, and (4) emphasizing the non-authoritative nature more clearly. The summary should be action-oriented and concise to help agents understand this is a permission-checking operation.

data.current_description: Enables a SecretVersion. Sets the state of the SecretVersion to ENABLED.
data.description_suggestion: Enables a SecretVersion by setting its state to ENABLED. This makes the secret version available for access. Requires the secret version to be in a disabled state. Returns the updated SecretVersion resource.
data.reasoning: Missing summary entirely. Description is minimal and lacks context about prerequisites, effects, and return value. Added information about state requirements and return value to improve AI agent understanding of when and how to use this operation.

message: Operation 'secretmanager.projects.secrets.list' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, vague_description, missing_output_details. Missing summary and extremely vague description. data.current_description: Creates a new SecretVersion containing secret data and attaches it to an existing Secret.

data.current_description: Gets the access control policy for a secret. Returns empty policy if the secret exists and does not have a policy set.
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves the Identity and Access Management (IAM) policy for a specified Secret. Returns the Policy resource containing role bindings and permissions. If no policy has been set, returns an empty Policy object. Used to determine who has access to the secret.
data.reasoning: Missing summary. Description mentions empty policy behavior but lacks context about the return type and the purpose of retrieving the policy. Added information about Policy structure and use case for better AI agent understanding.

message: Operation 'secretmanager.projects.secrets.setIamPolicy' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Missing summary. Description explains replacement behavior and version inheritance but lacks explicit return type and clarity about immediate effect. Added return type and emphasized immediate enforcement for better AI agent understanding of side effects.

data.current_description: Sets the access control policy on the specified secret. Replaces any existing policy. Permissions on SecretVersions are enforced according to the policy set on the associated Secret.
data.description_suggestion: Sets the Identity and Access Management (IAM) policy for a specified Secret, replacing any existing policy. The policy controls access to the secret and all its versions. Returns the updated Policy resource. Changes take effect immediately and affect all subsequent access attempts to the secret and its versions.
data.reasoning: Missing summary. Description explains replacement behavior and version inheritance but lacks explicit return type and clarity about immediate effect. Added return type and emphasized immediate enforcement for better AI agent understanding of side effects.

message: Operation 'secretmanager.projects.secrets.delete' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, vague_description, missing_input_details, missing_side_effects, poor_ai_interpretability. Original description is too minimal. Missing critical information about permanence, what gets deleted (all versions), required input format, return type, and side effects. AI agents need to understand this is a destructive operation with no recovery option.

data.summary_suggestion: Delete a secret and all its versions
data.description_suggestion: Permanently deletes a Secret resource and all of its SecretVersions. This operation cannot be undone. Requires the secret resource name (format: projects/{project}/secrets/{secret}). Returns an Empty response on success. All associated secret data is irrevocably destroyed.
data.reasoning: Original description is too minimal. Missing critical information about permanence, what gets deleted (all versions), required input format, return type, and side effects. AI agents need to understand this is a destructive operation with no recovery option.

message: Operation 'secretmanager.projects.secrets.versions.get' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_input_details, missing_output_details. Summary missing entirely. Description lacks clarity about what metadata is returned, what is NOT returned (secret data), required input format, and response type. AI agents need to distinguish this from the access operation.

data.current_description: Gets metadata for a SecretVersion. `projects/*/secrets/*/versions/latest` is an alias to the `latest` SecretVersion.
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves metadata for a specific SecretVersion, including creation time, state, and replication status. Does not return the secret data itself. Accepts the SecretVersion resource name (format: projects/{project}/secrets/{secret}/versions/{version}). The special alias `projects/*/secrets/*/versions/latest` retrieves the most recent SecretVersion. Returns a SecretVersion object containing metadata only.
data.reasoning: Summary missing entirely. Description lacks clarity about what metadata is returned, what is NOT returned (secret data), required input format, and response type. AI agents need to distinguish this from the access operation.

data.current_description: Updates metadata of an existing Secret.
data.description_suggestion: Updates metadata of an existing Secret resource, such as labels, replication policy, or rotation configuration. Requires the Secret resource name (format: projects/{project}/secrets/{secret}) and a Secret object with the fields to update. Supports partial updates - only specified fields are modified. Returns the updated Secret object with all current metadata.

data.current_description: Lists information about the supported locations for this service.
data.description_suggestion: Lists all Google Cloud locations where the Secret Manager service is available. Returns location information including display names and region details. Supports pagination through page_size and page_token parameters. Useful for determining where secrets can be replicated or created.

message: Operation 'secretmanager.projects.secrets.versions.access' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Summary missing. Description lacks clarity about the distinction from metadata retrieval, required input format, response type, and audit logging side effect. AI agents must understand this returns actual secret data and has security/audit implications.

data.current_description: Accesses a SecretVersion. This call returns the secret data. `projects/*/secrets/*/versions/latest` is an alias to the `latest` SecretVersion.
data.summary_suggestion: Retrieve secret data from a version
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves the secret data payload from a specific SecretVersion. This operation returns the actual sensitive data (unlike the get operation which returns only metadata). Requires the SecretVersion resource name (format: projects/{project}/secrets/{secret}/versions/{version}). The special alias `projects/*/secrets/*/versions/latest` retrieves data from the most recent SecretVersion. Returns an AccessSecretVersionResponse containing the secret payload. Access is logged for audit purposes.
data.reasoning: Summary missing. Description lacks clarity about the distinction from metadata retrieval, required input format, response type, and audit logging side effect. AI agents must understand this returns actual secret data and has security/audit implications.

message: Operation 'secretmanager.projects.secrets.versions.destroy' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_input_details, missing_output_details. Summary missing. Description lacks input format details, return type, and state transition information. AI agents need to understand the permanent nature, which states can be destroyed, and what the response contains.

data.current_description: Destroys a SecretVersion. Sets the state of the SecretVersion to DESTROYED and irrevocably destroys the secret data.
data.description_suggestion: Permanently destroys a SecretVersion, making it unusable and irrevocably deleting its secret data. Sets the SecretVersion state to DESTROYED. This operation cannot be undone. Requires the SecretVersion resource name (format: projects/{project}/secrets/{secret}/versions/{version}) and a DestroySecretVersionRequest. Returns a SecretVersion object with state set to DESTROYED. Only versions in ENABLED or DISABLED state can be destroyed.
data.reasoning: Summary missing. Description lacks input format details, return type, and state transition information. AI agents need to understand the permanent nature, which states can be destroyed, and what the response contains.

message: Operation 'secretmanager.projects.secrets.versions.disable' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_input_details, missing_output_details. Summary missing. Description lacks input format, return type, reversibility information, and state transition constraints. AI agents need to understand this is non-destructive and can be reversed, distinguishing it from destroy.

data.current_description: Disables a SecretVersion. Sets the state of the SecretVersion to DISABLED.
data.description_suggestion: Disables a SecretVersion, preventing it from being accessed while preserving the secret data. Sets the SecretVersion state to DISABLED. This operation is reversible via the enable operation. Requires the SecretVersion resource name (format: projects/{project}/secrets/{secret}/versions/{version}) and a DisableSecretVersionRequest. Returns a SecretVersion object with state set to DISABLED. Only ENABLED versions can be disabled.
data.reasoning: Summary missing. Description lacks input format, return type, reversibility information, and state transition constraints. AI agents need to understand this is non-destructive and can be reversed, distinguishing it from destroy.
